title: Container stats for NetIO swapped
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

I'm not sure if this is a bug or me misinterpreting the output. A containers _NetIO_ input and output stats seem mixed up to me. Running a command like `podman stats <container> --no-stream --format 'table {{.NetInput}}'` shows the overall outgoing traffic of a container instead of the incoming traffic as i would expect. Looking at the [stats code](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/b13939bb6f3301a2ac0c0fd3eed5dcfca3444b7a/libpod/stats_linux.go#L75) shows:

```go
stats.NetInput = netStats.TxBytes
stats.NetOutput = netStats.RxBytes
```

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. `podman run -d --rm --name netstats alpine sh -c "wget https://speed.hetzner.de/100MB.bin && sleep 60"`
2. `podman stats netstats --no-stream --format 'table {{.NetInput}} {{.NetOutput}}'`

**Describe the results you received:**
```
NET INPUT   NET OUTPUT
705835      108906127
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
```
NET INPUT   NET OUTPUT
108906127      705835
```

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.3.0
API Version:  4.3.0
Go Version:   go1.18.7
Built:        Fri Oct 21 08:16:35 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

comments.body: Yes I think you are right. Do you want to open a PR with the fix?
Sure, i'll give it a try. It might take me a moment to setup a dev environment though.
